jobs.title: Senior Wealth Strategy Consultant
jobs.description:
Job DescriptionOne of WFS Group’s rapidly growing financial services clients is seeking highly consultative CFP® professionals to join their inbound sales team and help high net worth retirees make smarter retirement tax planning decisions.

This is NOT a traditional financial advisor role.

You will NOT be:- Prospecting- Cold calling- Networking for referrals- Managing assets- Building financial plans- Hunting for clientsInstead, you’ll spend your time having sophisticated consultative conversations with affluent retirees and pre-retirees who have already expressed interest in advanced retirement tax planning strategies.

This is a high-ticket inbound consultative sales role designed for CFP® professionals who genuinely enjoy client conversations, relationship-building, education, and helping prospects make informed financial decisions.

The ideal candidate is not a “hard closer.”

They are a trusted advisor, a confident communicator, and someone who can create genuine “aha moments” during the conversation.

If you’ve ever thought:- “I’m great with clients but tired of building a book”

- “I love the conversations more than the planning work”

- “I’d earn significantly more if someone handed me qualified meetings”
…this may be exactly the opportunity you’ve been looking for.

A Snapshot of WFS Group:WFS Group is a fast-paced, high-performance outsourced sales organization specializing in helping companies scale through world-class sales talent and infrastructure.

Think of us as an outsourced sales department for fast-growing businesses.

Our clients partner with us to recruit, train, and develop elite sales professionals who can represent their brands at the highest level. We work with innovative companies across multiple industries that rely on consultative sales environments and premium client experiences.

This opportunity is with one of our premier financial services partners specializing in retirement tax planning and wealth strategy solutions.

Position Overview:As a Senior Wealth Strategy Consultant, you will conduct 25-30 pre-scheduled Zoom consultations per week with high-net-worth prospects generated directly through the client’s marketing engine.

These are warm, inbound conversations with individuals typically:

- Ages 59-75- Holding $1M-$5M+ in retirement assets- Actively seeking retirement tax planning guidance

Using the client’s proven consultative framework, you’ll assess each prospect’s retirement situation, educate them on advanced tax planning strategies, and help them determine whether the process is the right fit.

You are the closer, not the planner.A dedicated planning team handles implementation after the client enrolls.

This role is ideal for professionals who:

- Communicate with confidence- Build trust naturally- Simplify complex financial concepts- Thrive in relationship-driven conversations

- Enjoy educating more than “selling”- Want the earning upside of sales without the grind of prospecting

Why Top Performers Love This Opportunity:- 100% remote environment- 25-30 inbound consultations per week provided

- $200K-$250K+ realistic earning potential- No cold calling or prospecting- Sophisticated financial conversations with affluent clients

- High-ticket consultative sales environment

- Extensive coaching and ongoing development

- High-performance culture without toxic sales pressure

- Opportunity to sell a service that genuinely changes lives

Major Roles & Responsibilities:- Conduct inbound Zoom consultations with qualified prospects.

- Use the client’s diagnostic framework to assess retirement planning concerns and tax exposure risks.

- Educate prospects on retirement tax mitigation and Roth conversion planning concepts.

- Create meaningful “epiphany moments” that help prospects understand the long-term impact of tax planning decisions.

- Build trust and credibility through clear, consultative communication.

- Guide prospects through the decision-making process without relying on pressure-based sales tactics.

- Manage follow-up conversations and pipeline activity with discipline and consistency.

- Maintain accurate CRM documentation and forecasting.

- Participate in coaching sessions, team reviews, and ongoing training initiatives.

- Consistently achieve or exceed revenue and close-rate targets.

- Contribute positively to a high-performance, coachable team culture.

Professionals Who Tend To Thrive Here:- CFP® professionals who enjoy client conversations more than building plans

- Advisors tired of prospecting and business development

- Consultants who naturally create trust and clarity

- Professionals with strong executive presence on Zoom

- Financial professionals who enjoy educating more than “selling”

- Relationship-driven communicators with strong emotional intelligence

- Advisors who want to maximize income without managing assets

- Highly coachable professionals who thrive within proven systems

Requirements:- CFP® designation in good standing (non-negotiable)

- 5+ years in a client-facing financial planning, advisory, or consultative financial services role

- Experience working with high-net-worth individuals

- Strong consultative communication skills- Clean U4 regulatory history- Confidence presenting premium planning fees

- Strong executive presence and professionalism on Zoom

- CRM experience preferredYou SHOULD Apply If:- You naturally build trust quickly with people

- You’re confident without being overly aggressive

You SHOULD NOT Apply If:- You prefer technical planning work over client interaction

If you’re a high-performing CFP® professional who loves consultative client conversations but is tired of the traditional advisor grind, this is an opportunity to maximize both your impact and earning potential in a highly differentiated role.

jobs.title: Azure AI / Copilot Engineer (m/w/d) – Consulting Crew
jobs.description:
deine aufgabenBei WorkSimple entsteht eine neue Azure AI & Copilot Unit, die sich auf die praktische Umsetzung moderner KI-Lösungen im Microsoft-Ökosystem konzentriert. Unser Ziel ist es, Künstliche Intelligenz gezielt zur Verbesserung realer Prozesse einzusetzen. Dabei setzen wir auf einen pragmatischen, nutzerzentrierten Ansatz: Wir beobachten Abläufe direkt im Arbeitsumfeld der Anwender, identifizieren Optimierungspotenziale und entwickeln daraus konkrete Lösungen.

Als Azure AI / Copilot Engineer & Consultant arbeitest du an der Schnittstelle von Cloud Engineering, KI-Integration und Prozessverständnis. Du entwickelst, integrierst und betreibst Azure AI- und Copilot-Lösungen, die messbaren Nutzen für unsere Kunden schaffen, und gestaltest gleichzeitig den Aufbau dieser neuen Abteilung aktiv mit.

Im Detail heißt das:

- Entwicklung und Implementierung von Azure AI- und Copilot-Lösungen auf Basis von Azure OpenAI, Cognitive Services, AI Search und Copilot Studio• Technische Integration von Copilot Studio in Microsoft 365, Power Platform oder kundenspezifische Anwendungen- Aufbau von RAG-Szenarien, Knowledge Bases und KI-Workflows zur Nutzung von Unternehmensdaten in Chatbots oder digitalen Assistenten- Entwicklung von Azure Functions, Logic Apps, APIs und Automatisierungen zur Anbindung externer Systeme- Entwicklung von MVPs und Quick-Win-Lösungen, die schnell echten Mehrwert erzeugen

dein profilDu bist ein technikaffiner Problemlöser mit einem Verständnis für Unternehmensprozesse und Begeisterung für Künstliche Intelligenz. Du möchtest nicht nur beraten, sondern funktionierende Lösungen entwickeln und aktiv gestalten, wie WorkSimple KI im Mittelstand einsetzt.

Das bringst du mit:

- Mehrjährige Erfahrung in der Entwicklung mit Microsoft Azure / M365- Kenntnisse in Azure AI Services, Azure OpenAI, Cognitive Services oder Copilot Studio- Programmierkenntnisse in Python, C# oder TypeScript- Verständnis für KI-Architekturen, Datenflüsse, RAG-Konzepte und generative KI- Interesse an LLMs, Prompt Engineering und der Integration von unternehmensspezifischem Wissen in KI-Systeme- Kommunikationsstärke und die Fähigkeit, technische Inhalte klar und praxisnah zu vermitteln- Zertifizierungen wie AI-102, AZ-204, AZ-305 oder PL-400 sind von Vorteil- Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (C1)Warum WorkSimple und warum ich, Robin, dich suche:

Du arbeitest in einer neuen Abteilung, die du aktiv mitgestalten kannst. Wir möchten Künstliche Intelligenz so einsetzen, dass sie Menschen in ihrem Arbeitsalltag spürbar unterstützt und Aufgaben einfacher macht. Dabei verbinden wir das technische Know-how unseres Cloud-Teams mit einer offenen, bodenständigen Arbeitsweise.

Bei uns kannst du dein Wissen in Azure, AI und Automatisierung direkt in Projekten einsetzen und mit uns gemeinsam Lösungen entwickeln, die wirklich funktionieren. Wir arbeiten eng zusammen, tauschen uns regelmäßig aus und helfen uns gegenseitig, besser zu werden.arbeiten bei der worksimpleIch bin Robin und seit fünf Jahren Teil von WorkSimple und verantworte den Aufbau unserer neuen Azure AI & Copilot-Abteilung. Wir sind ein kleines, erfahrenes Team, das Freude daran hat, Dinge anzupacken und Neues auszuprobieren. Unsere Kundenbasis ist da, es gibt viele interessante Projekte. Was uns noch fehlt, ist jemand, der die technische Umsetzung mit vorantreibt und gemeinsam mit uns Erfahrungen sammelt.

Wir suchen keine Perfektion, sondern Neugier und den Wunsch, gemeinsam etwas aufzubauen. Wenn du Spaß an Technologie hast und gerne im Team arbeitest, findest du bei uns den richtigen Platz, um dich einzubringen und etwas zu bewegen.

Beispiele aus unseren Kundenwünschen:

- Entwicklung von Copilot-Agenten für interne Wissensplattformen und Prozessunterstützung- Aufbau von KI-basierten Dokumenten- und Ticket-Assistenten mit Azure OpenAI und Form Recognizer- Integration von Copilot Studio in Microsoft Teams und die Power Platform- Entwicklung von RAG-Workflows für Wissensmanagement und Supportprozesse- Predictive Maintenance und Prozessautomatisierung auf Basis von Machine-Learning-Modellen- Quick-Win-Prototypen für Aufgaben wie E-Mail-Klassifikation, Berichtszusammenfassung oder automatisierte Kommunikation

jobs.title: Chief of Staff
jobs.description:
About A1Our mission is to help in integrating intelligence to the world. A1 is building a proactive AI smart assistant for everyday users to bring intelligence to conversations, errands, organising and workflows, with minimal prompting.

Our product focuses on achieving high reliability for long-running workflows, persistent context, and real-world task completion. The system must handle multi-step reasoning, interact with external tools, and remain reliable despite non-deterministic model behavior.

About the RoleYou will be responsible for building and running the operational infrastructure behind A1.

This includes setting up new offices and entities, implementing payroll and local operations, managing vendors, supporting recruiting operations, and ensuring the company can execute efficiently as it scales.

You will be responsible for operational execution across markets.

What You Will Be Doing- Lead business operations across multiple countries.

- Manage and develop a team of Business Operations Partners.

- Oversee office setup, entity incorporation, and market launches.

- Oversee payroll implementation and local operational requirements.

- Manage external vendors and service providers.

- Support recruiting operations, recruiter onboarding, and hiring activities.

- Plan and execute company events, offsites, and operational logistics.

- Track key initiatives and ensure actions are completed on time.

- Improve processes where needed to increase speed and reliability.

What You Will Need- Experience in business operations, strategic operations, founder's office, or similar roles.

- Experience managing projects involving multiple stakeholders.

- Strong organisational skills and attention to detail.

- Ability to manage multiple priorities and follow through on commitments.

- Comfortable working across recruiting, finance, legal, and administrative functions.

- Able to identify problems, make decisions, and drive execution independently.

- High ownership and willingness to take responsibility for outcomes.

How We WorkThe best products in the world are built by small, highly capable teams.

We operate with high talent density, high ownership and high standards. We value people who can think independently, move quickly and solve problems without waiting for instructions.

This is a hands-on environment. Everyone is expected to contribute directly to the company's success.

Interview ProcessIf there appears to be a fit, we will schedule up to 3 interviews.

Applications are reviewed by members of our leadership and hiring teams. Interviews may be conducted virtually or onsite.

We value speed, transparency and decisiveness throughout the process.

jobs.title: AI Engineer (Technical Product Owner)
jobs.location: Albania, Andorra, Armenia, Belgium, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Bulgaria, Croatia, Czechia, Estonia, France, Georgia, Greece, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Malta, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Poland, Slovenia
jobs.description:
At Foxelli, we’re building more than e-commerce brands - we’re building a tribe of thoughtful humans: AI enthusiasts, curious builders, and ambitious minds who care about doing meaningful work together. Ten years in, our D2C brands reach millions and generate over $20M annually. Our focus stays simple: create products customers love and a team that actually enjoys building them.

If you love to learn, experiment, think boldly, make decisions, and build things that matter - you’ll feel at home with us.

WHY JOIN FOXELLI?We are a fast-moving e-commerce company that builds our own proprietary AI infrastructure. We develop internal AI-driven systems to automate complex creative and analytical workflows, which we later scale into B2B SaaS products.
We are not looking for a traditional developer. 

We are looking for an AI-Native Builder who can turn an idea into a functional prototype in days using the latest AI-assisted development tools.

THE ROLEAs a Founding AI Engineer, you will work directly with the founder to design, build, and iterate on various AI systems. The projects will vary - from computer vision and image manipulation to automated video generation and agentic data analysis. You will be responsible for the full pipeline: from R&D of the best AI models to deploying scalable web applications.

Who You Are:

- Modern Developer: You have abandoned "manual-only" coding. You are a power user of tools like GPT Codex, Claude code, and Replit, allowing you to build at a 10x pace.

- AI Generalist: You are comfortable working across different AI domains - LLMs (Text/Reasoning), Diffusion (Image/Video), and Vision models.

- Agile & Adaptive: You thrive in an environment where projects evolve. You are a fast learner who can master a new AI framework or API over a weekend.

- Hacker Mindset: You focus on ROI. You build lean MVPs, test them in our real-world e-commerce operations, and only then polish them for scale.

- Architecture-Aware: You know how to orchestrate multiple AI models into a single "Agentic" workflow that handles complex tasks autonomously.

Technical Interests:- Stack: Next.js / Python (FastAPI).

- AI Frameworks: ComfyUI / Diffusers, IP-Adapters, ControlNet, and Agentic frameworks (LangChain/CrewAI).

- Infrastructure: Experience with Serverless GPU providers (Replicate, Fal, RunPod).

- Integrations: Skilled at connecting diverse APIs to create seamless automated systems.

Why Join Us:- Direct Impact: Build tools that are immediately put to use in real-world business scenarios.

- Extreme Speed: No corporate red tape. If the tech works, we ship it.

- Evolution: This is a founding role with the opportunity to lead multiple product lines as we transition from internal tools to SaaS.

THE RECRUITMENT PROCESS- Apply - A simple form that helps us know who’s stepping into our world.

-  Ribbon AI Interview -  Ribbon is a short, guided video step that helps us understand your thinking style in a calm, private space

- Interview - A conversation with your manager to feel the work chemistry, get to know each other, and explore what we could build together

FIXED SERVICE FEE€2000- €3000 after taxes (depending on yourskills, competencies, and experience).

PERKS & BENEFITS- Full flexibility with your schedule in a fully remote setting, while Apploye ensures transparency and fairness.

- Complimentary gym memberships and company-sponsored outdoor adventures for your strength and to keep your physical well-being in check.

- A personal development budget is at your disposal for your personal and professional growth to ensure you keep growing continuously.

- Generous paid time off, including parental leave and vacation days, to recharge and rejuvenate and ensure you’re well-rested.

- A budget for your home office setup so you’re comfortable working how you like and where you like.

- Strong company culture and a network of unique individuals that celebrate you.

ELIGIBILITY & LOCATION REQUIREMENTS❕This role is 100% remote.- Our tribe works primarily in the Eastern European Time Zone (EET - UTC+2/UTC+3). We also use Revolut for payments, so we are open to hiring candidates in countries where Revolut payments are supported.

- For full-time roles, we ask that you’re available for at least 5 hours during typical EET working hours - it keeps collaboration smooth and communication easy.

(This doesn’t apply to part-time roles.)Currently, we are hiring in Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Croatia, Czech Republic, Estonia, Greece, Georgia, Kosovo, Latvia, Lithuania, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Malta, Slovenia, Serbia, Cyprus, Bulgaria, Hungary, Netherlands, the United Kingdom, and South Africa.

jobs.title: CRM Coordinator
jobs.description:
About UsInflection Club is a private co-investment community for accredited investors, operators, and family offices who want direct access to pre-IPO deals in the companies shaping the future. Built under Catalyst Capital Holdings, we provide curated access from early to late-stage opportunities that traditionally only flow to endowments, pension funds, and institutional allocators - names like Anthropic, Polymarket, Kraken, and Lovable.

Our thesis is simple: the biggest returns of the next decade will come from companies built on the cost curves of artificial intelligence, robotics, longevity, and next-generation infrastructure. We help our members invest in those companies 12 to 18 months before they go public, deal-by-deal, with full discretion.

We are not a fund. We are a club. No blind pools, no lock-ups, no marketing dressed up as deal flow. Every member chooses what they back, and we handle everything else - sourcing, diligence, SPV formation, legal, reporting, and the live community that makes the whole thing run.

What You Will Do:The CRM Coordinator is the operational backbone of our member and prospect pipeline. You will own the integrity and performance of our CRM - ensuring every contact, deal stage, and communication touchpoint is accurate, timed, and purposeful.

This is not a data-entry role. We need someone who understands why the data matters, takes ownership of process breakdowns before anyone else notices them, and continuously improves how we track and engage our community. You will work closely with the Director of Community & Operations to ensure no relationship falls through the cracks.

What You'll Own1. CRM Administration & Data Integrity- Own day-to-day administration of HubSpot: contact records, deal stages, pipeline accuracy, custom properties, and lifecycle stages.- Maintain a single source of truth for all member and prospect data - clean, current, and actionable.- Proactively identify and resolve duplicate records, data anomalies, and process gaps before they surface as problems.2. Workflows, Sequences & Automation- Build and maintain workflows, sequences, and automations that support member acquisition, engagement, and retention.- Coordinate with the Partner/Origination and Managing Partner teams to ensure pipeline data reflects real-time activity.- Continuously improve the CRM architecture as the club scales - new pipelines, new stages, new segments.3. Reporting & Funnel Insights- Track and report on key funnel metrics: applications, conversions, member tenure, engagement signals, and re-engagement triggers.- Surface insights to leadership in a clear, concise format - no noise, just signal.- Build and maintain dashboards that give the team visibility into pipeline health at a glance.4. List Segmentation & Campaign Coordination

- Own audience segmentation and list management for outreach campaigns coordinated with the Director of Community & Operations.- Ensure the right message reaches the right segment at the right time - and that results are tracked cleanly.5. Member Onboarding Flows- Support the operational layer of new member onboarding - ensuring the right communications go out on schedule and that member records are updated as they move through each stage.- Liaise with the Director of Community & Operations to keep onboarding workflows current and optimised.Requirements

Who You Are:

- 2+ years of hands-on CRM or revenue operations experience, with HubSpot as a hard requirement.- You have a strong grasp of sales pipeline management, lifecycle stages, and funnel reporting - not just where to find them in a dashboard, but what they mean.- You can build workflows and sequences without hand-holding. You know when automation helps and when it creates more problems than it solves.- You have exceptional attention to detail. You notice when something is off, and you fix it before being asked.- You are async-first and comfortable operating in a fast-moving, distributed team across time zones.- You communicate clearly and concisely - in writing, always.Bonus Points- Experience in financial services, investment communities, high-ticket sales, or private membership organisations.- Familiarity with Web3, DeFi, or crypto-adjacent audiences.- Exposure to SPV workflows, investor relations, or regulated communications.- Experience with ClickUp, Slack automation, or n8n.How We Work- Inflection Club's leadership operates on a Yin-Yang model borrowed from how our founders built Decentralized Masters. Every major initiative gets multiple sets of eyes before it ships. You will work closely with the Director of Community & Operations and surface issues early. You will own your domain. And you will be expected to set the quality bar - not wait for it to be set for you.- We move quickly. We do not tolerate sloppiness. We expect the CRM to be a tool that makes the team faster and sharper, not a system people work around.Benefits- Fully remote - work from anywhere.- Flexible, async-first culture with deep focus time built in.- Competitive independent contractor rate commensurate with experience.- Direct exposure to institutional-grade deal flow and a high-calibre investor community.- Ground-floor opportunity in a private co-investment club being built to scale.Are You Ready?This is a rare opportunity to own the operational intelligence layer of a private investment community that is growing fast and playing for the long game. If you are the kind of person who finds broken processes and fixes them before being asked - and who wants to be part of something being built to last - we want to hear from you.

Apply with your CV in English, along with a brief note on the CRM system you know best and one process improvement you have owned end-to-end. We move quickly and will be in touch with shortlisted candidates within two weeks.
